transformation_type: condensation of alpha-amino ketones with ketones or active methylene compounds to substituted pyrroles
mechanism_type: imine/enamine formation and cyclization of alpha-amino ketones with carbonyl partners after in situ reduction or preparation of the amino ketone
reactant_pattern_text: alpha-amino ketone + ketone or active methylene compound
common_conditions: often nitrosation/reduction to alpha-amino ketone followed by heating or reflux to promote cyclization
description_short: Classical pyrrole synthesis that converts alpha-amino ketones and carbonyl partners into highly substituted pyrroles through condensation, enamine formation, and ring closure.

transformation_type: glycosidation of glycosyl halides with alcohols or phenols under heavy-metal salts or Lewis-acid activation
mechanism_type: formation of glycosyl oxocarbenium or dioxolanium ions from glycosyl halides followed by nucleophilic substitution with stereochemical control
reactant_pattern_text: glycosyl chloride or bromide + alcohol/phenol nucleophile
common_conditions: typically low temperature to room temperature with activated glycosyl halide and silver or mercury promoter
description_short: Foundational glycosidation method using glycosyl halides and metal-salt or Lewis-acid promoters to form O-glycosides with substrate- and neighboring-group-controlled stereochemistry.

transformation_type: carboxylation of phenoxide salts with carbon dioxide to salicylic-acid-type aromatic hydroxy acids
mechanism_type: CO2 insertion into alkali-metal phenoxides via phenoxide-CO2 complexes followed by thermal rearrangement or direct aromatic carboxylation
reactant_pattern_text: phenol or substituted phenol converted to alkali-metal phenoxide + CO2
product_pattern_text: ortho- or para-hydroxy aromatic carboxylic acid
common_conditions: thermal or pressurized carboxylation of dried phenoxide salts, often above 100 °C
description_short: Aromatic carboxylation method that transforms phenoxides into salicylic-acid or para-hydroxybenzoic-acid derivatives under CO2, heat, and base.

transformation_type: oxidation of activated alkyl halides or alpha-halo carbonyl compounds to aldehydes, ketones, or alpha-oxo carbonyl compounds by DMSO
mechanism_type: nucleophilic substitution of halide by DMSO to alkoxysulfonium salts followed by deprotonation and elimination or rearrangement to carbonyl products
reactant_pattern_text: activated alkyl halide or alpha-halo carbonyl compound in DMSO with base or silver salt
common_conditions: often heated DMSO under basic or silver-assisted conditions; room-temperature Ganem variants with amine N-oxides are also used
description_short: Oxidation platform that converts activated halides into carbonyl compounds through DMSO-derived alkoxysulfonium intermediates under basic, silver-assisted, or N-oxide-mediated conditions.

transformation_type: salt-promoted removal of alkoxycarbonyl groups from malonic, beta-keto, alpha-cyano, and related activated esters
mechanism_type: cyanide or halide salt promoted dealkoxycarbonylation/decarboxylation in wet polar aprotic solvent via nucleophilic substitution or carbonyl addition pathways
reactant_pattern_text: malonic ester|beta-keto ester|alpha-cyano ester + NaCN/KCN/LiCl/NaCl in wet polar aprotic solvent
common_conditions: usually heated wet polar aprotic solvent with at least one equivalent of salt; esters lost as alcohol or alkyl halide byproducts
description_short: Dealkoxycarbonylation method that removes methoxycarbonyl or ethoxycarbonyl groups from activated esters under neutral salt-promoted conditions in wet polar aprotic solvents.
